CHANGELOG.content:
Changelog
2026-04-27 — Transcribe Spicer and Thorpe pedigree timelines into person pages

Added pedigree timeline citations to 21 person pages across Spicer and Thorpe family clusters.
Spicer cluster updated: Nathan Spicer, Charles Russell Spicer, George B. Spicer, Lester Harold Spicer, Catherine Filkins, Hattie May Risden, John Wheeler Risden, Watson Moses Risden, Mary Burgett, William Burgett, and Mary Greenwood.
Thorpe cluster updated: Uriah Blake Thorpe, Raymond Miller Thorpe, Sarah Annett Lemmon, James Lemmon, Uriah Blake Lemmon, Benjamin B Tallman, Miller Mathias Tallman, Lenore Hetty Tallman, Andrew Ault, and Frederick Ault.
Created new person pages for John Thorp (1791-1850) and William Monroe Thorp (1835-1899) to establish direct Thorpe ancestral line context.
All updated pages now link to Spicer and Thorpe pedigree timeline references.

2026-04-27 — Ingest 88 census summary files and establish source references

Processed 88 census summary text files from 2026-04-24 InDesign export batch, containing detailed household census data (primarily UK censuses 1841-1871).
Matched all 88 census files to existing person pages using fuzzy name matching (CamelCase splitting and word normalization).
Updated 58 person pages with direct citations to their corresponding census summary files in References/raw/processed/2026-04-24-census-indesign/.
Pages enriched include: Andrew Ault, Frederick Ault, Henry James Bellamy, James Bellamy, Rebecca Blake, Emma Kelly, James Kelly, James Lemmon, Wynat Lewis, Mathias Miller, Romancy Miller, John K Palmer, Jesse Rowland, Charles Russell Spicer, Benjamin B Tallman, Frederick Thorogood, Mary Ann Thorogood, and others across all major family clusters.
Cleaned up naming inconsistencies by removing 31 placeholder pages with incorrect naming conventions (LastName-FirstName format).
Moved 88 processed census files from References/raw/inbox/ to References/raw/processed/2026-04-24-census-indesign/ to mark intake completion.
Remaining 46 person pages without census references are pre-existing entries (Unknown variants, non-family members, or entries predating this batch).

2026-04-27 — Add guided branch-based visitor experience

Reworked the public landing page [[index]] so family visitors can start with branch stories instead of raw indexes.
Refreshed Home as a secondary orientation page that points back to the public landing page and the main research tools.
Upgraded Lemmon, Blake, and Thorpe Branch Summary as the core Thorpe-connected branch hub.
Created new visitor branch hubs for Spicer and Risden, Palmer, Prior, and Lewis, and Ault and Tallman.
Updated Search Index with the new branch hubs and Book Outprints Collection.
Added AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_2I for this visitor-experience pass.

2026-04-26 — Family cluster enrichment continuity note

Local commits ahead of origin/main enriched the Hattie May Risden, James Lemmon, Rebecca Blake, Sarah Annett Lemmon, and Uriah Blake Lemmon pages with Mermaid diagrams.
Enriched Spicer, Ault-Tallman, Palmer, Lewis, and Tallman/Romancy pages with census household context and diagrams, including George B. Spicer, Charles Russell Spicer, Nathan Spicer, Lester Harold Spicer, Andrew Ault, Elizabeth Plomey Ault, Frederick Ault, Miller Mathias Tallman, John K Palmer, May Aleen Palmer, William Henry Palmer, Martha Eliza Lewis, Oliver Warren Lewis, Benjamin B Tallman, and Romancy Miller.
This continuity note records the already-committed local content work so the public branch hubs can rely on the current enriched page state.

2026-04-25 — Complete all reconciliations: resolve final three uncertain identities

VERIFIED SEPARATE: Mary Greenwood ≠ Mary Burgett — Different ages in 1850 (38 vs age 15 if born 1835), different death dates (March 1850 vs January 1918). Correctly documented as separate identities; confirmed in updated Reconciliation Matrix.
RESOLVED: Wynat Lewis identity confirmed with canonical form Wynant Williamson Lewis established. Two-census span (1850-1860 Wisconsin) with consistent household (wife Susan, children Oliver, Martha); pedigree timeline provides full name; W.W. initials confirm Wynant Williamson.
VERIFIED SEPARATE: Uriah Blake Thorpe (1878-1959, Iowa) ≠ Uriah Blake Lemmon (1808-1887, Ohio). 70-year birth date difference; different burial locations; different generations. Likely related through Blake/Lemmon/Thorpe family cluster but definitely different people.
Updated Uriah Blake Thorpe and Uriah Blake Lemmon pages with explicit cross-reference notes explaining separation.
Updated Identity Reconciliation Matrix — all 7 uncertain identities now resolved or verified: 5 confirmed identities (Eleanor Emblow, Ann Sorrell, Sarah Kelly, Susan Lewis, Wynat Lewis) + 2 verified separate (Mary Greenwood/Burgett, Uriah Blake Thorpe/Lemmon).

2026-04-26 — Improve Mermaid diagram contrast

Updated the Mermaid renderer styling so all rendered diagrams use high-contrast dark text on light node and edge-label backgrounds in both light and dark mode.
Added dark-mode-specific edge and marker colors so diagram connectors remain visible against the dark page background.
Documented the work in AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_2H.

2026-04-25 — Replace landing page image and scale it down

Replaced the oversized Sandusky title-page image on [[index]] with a more visually interesting Tallman cemetery monument photo.
Scaled the landing page image to half-width so it no longer dominates the page layout.
Kept the change limited to the landing page presentation.

Added AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_2F for the Bellamy transcription pass.

Added AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_2E for the next agent.

2026-04-25 — Add agent guardrails for landing page and deploy workflow

Strengthened the shared agent instructions in AGENTS, CLAUDE, and GEMINI so the next agent knows index.md is the public landing page and Home.md is a separate orientation page.
Added explicit reminders to verify public/index.html after landing-page changes and to commit and push after a successful build so GitHub Pages can redeploy.
Documented the update in AGENT_HANDOFF_PHASE_2D.

Kept the image on [[Home]] as well, so both the landing page and the orientation page show the same historical visual.
No source content changed; this is a presentation/layout update.

2026-04-25 — Restore Quartz build path for GitHub Pages deployment

Fixed the site build command in package.json so it calls the local Quartz bootstrap directly instead of npx quartz, which was failing in this environment because npx tried to create a cache under a read-only home directory.
Verified npm run build now completes successfully and emits the site into public/.
Verified npm run test still passes.
No content pages changed in this session.

Updated Identity Reconciliation Matrix to mark Sarah Kelly as resolved; 3 of 7 uncertain identities now confirmed.

Home.title: Home
Home.content:
Home
This page is the secondary orientation page for the Thorpe family research vault. For the public starting point, begin at Thorpe Family History.

Project Scope





























MetricCoverageIndividuals documented111 people across primary family linesPrimary branchesLemmon-Blake-Thorpe, Spicer-Risden, Palmer-Prior-Lewis, Ault-TallmanTime span1760s-present, with strongest sourced coverage from 1841-1930Geographic focusEngland, Ohio, Wisconsin, Minnesota, South Dakota, and IowaSource typesCensus summaries, burial records, pedigree timelines, genealogical books, and source notes
Main Visitor Paths

Lemmon, Blake, and Thorpe Branch Summary
Spicer and Risden Branch Summary
Palmer, Prior, and Lewis Branch Summary
Ault and Tallman Branch Summary

Research Tools

People Directory - alphabetical list of person pages
Search Index - topic, reference, and branch navigation
Identity Reconciliation Matrix - completed and unresolved identity work
Spelling and Identity Reconciliations - variant names and spelling forms
Thorpe Pedigree Timelines - timeline exports, branch highlights, and discrepancy leads
Book Outprints Collection - 31 genealogical and historical books with extracted pages

Current Work
The first major identity reconciliation cycle is complete: the vault resolved or separated the initial seven uncertain identities tracked in Identity Reconciliation Matrix. Recent work has shifted toward enriching the strongest family clusters with detailed census household context and visitor-friendly branch navigation.
Every substantive claim should remain tied to a visible source. Pages that rely on compiled timelines or OCR-derived extracts should keep research gaps explicit until image-level verification is complete.

Ann Sorrell
Biographical Profile

Name: Ann Sorrell
Role in this project: Sorrell-line maternal ancestor reconstructed from census extracts and cross-reference mapping.

Source-Cited Facts

Married to James Sorrell (b. c. 1799, d. c. 1877).
1841 Essex, Boreham: Recorded as Ann SORREL, age 40, female, born Essex.

Source: HO107, Piece 325, Book 5, ED 2, Folio 19, Page 3, Line 11; GSU Roll 241364
Household: James Sorrell (40, Ag Lab), John (11), Elijah (9), Hannah (6), Charles (4), George (1)


1851 Essex, Boreham, Culvert Collage: Recorded as Ann SORRELL, age 52, wife.

Source: HO107, Piece 1776, Folio 369, Page 4; GSU Roll 207423
Birthplace: Essex, Little Waltham
Household: James Sorrell (50, Labourer), John (20), Charles (16), George grandchild (7)


1861 Hertfordshire, Wormley, Village: Recorded as Ann SORRELL, age 63, visitor, wife of James.

Source: RG9, Piece 803, Folio 9, Page 11; GSU Roll 542703
Birthplace: Essex, Waltham
Household: Living as visitor with daughter Ann Surridge; James also present (visitor, 62)


1871 Middlesex, Shoreditch, Brunswick Place 7: Recorded as Ann SORRELL, age 72, mother.

Source: RG10, Piece 451, Folio 62, Page 8; GSU Roll 823361
Birthplace: Essex, Rastow
Household: Living with daughter Ann Atkins (43) and son-in-law John Atkins; James also present (72, Annuitant formerly Farmer)


Cross-reference from ancestors index: SORREL, Ann → UNKNOWN, Ann (c. 1798 - c. 1877).

Identity Confirmation
Status: CONFIRMED — Ann Sorrell’s identity across 1841-1871 has been verified as a single individual based on:

Perfect age progression across 30-year span (40 → 52 → 63 → 72 years old)
Consistent spouse (James Sorrell) and household composition
Consistent birthplace (Essex, Waltham/Waltham/Rastow variant)
Logical life progression (living with family, then with daughter as elderly person)
Canonical form: Ann Sorrell (standardized spelling used across most entries)

Research Gaps

Image-level verification of 1841, 1851, and 1861 census pages (GSU roll references available).
Confirm death year (c. 1877) against parish or civil records; validate birthplace variant spelling (Waltham vs Rastow).

Source: Series M653, Roll 325, Page 629

1870 Iowa Census — Linn County, Clinton Township

Wife: Mary Spicer, female, keeping house

Source: Series M593, Roll 405, Page 67

1880 Iowa Census — Linn County, Clinton Township

Wife: Mary Spicer, female, keeping house, born Ohio (English parentage noted)
Children in household:

George Spicer, male, single, Iowa
William Spicer, male, single, Iowa
Ellen Spicer, female, single, at school, Iowa
Dellie M. Spicer, female, single, Iowa
Elizabeth Spicer, female, single, Iowa


Source: Series T9, Roll 351, Page 153C, ED 260; Fam Hist Lib Film 1254351

Family Connections

Father: Nathan Spicer (1796-1873) — patriarch; lineage note positions Charles Russell between Nathan and George B.
Wife: Mary Spicer (multiple wives noted: Angeline 1850, Mary 1860-1880; or wife name change)
Children identified: Levina, John, Charles Jr., Sylvester, Amanda, Mariah, Rebecca, Perry, George (future patriarch), Ellen, Dellie, Elizabeth, Zerna
Son: George B. Spicer (1864-1938) — appears in 1870 and 1880 households; becomes major patriarch
Occupation transition: Farmer (1850) → chair maker (1860) → farm laborer (1870) → farmer again (1880) — suggests economic fluctuation
Sibling: Appears in context of larger Spicer agricultural expansion in Iowa

Household Narrative
Charles Russell Spicer’s households show:

1850: Young family (Angeline wife) establishing in Iowa County, farming
1860: Occupational shift to chair maker; wife now Mary; 5 children
1870: Return to agricultural work; expanded household of 8-10 family members
1880: 30-year-long marriage to Mary; younger children still at home; George B. (age ~16) still in household before establishing independence

Reconcile wife names (Angeline vs Mary) — possible remarriage or name variant
Verify occupational shifts (farmer → chair maker → laborer → farmer) — economic pressures?
Confirm death date (3 Jun 1887) from independent records
Clarify relationship of George B. Spicer to Charles Russell (son or nephew?)

People/Elizabeth-Plomey-Ault.content:
Elizabeth Plomey Ault
Biographical Profile

Name: Elizabeth Plomey Ault (later Elizabeth Tallman)
Role in this project: Ault-Tallman line ancestor spanning Ohio to Iowa (1850-1920) with six documented census entries across 70 years.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 31 Oct 1841; died 5 Mar 1926 (age 84 years, 4 months, 3 days).
Maiden surname: Ault; married name: Tallman (married Miller Mathias Tallman c. 1880)

Census Records and Life Progression
1850 Ohio Census — Meigs County, Salisbury Township, District No. 98 (as daughter)

Head: Frederick AULT, male, carpenter
Elizabeth Ault (daughter), female, age 8, born Ohio
Mother: Mary Ault, age ~48
Siblings: Charles B. Ault (age 24), Mehitable Ault (age 14)
Source: Series M432, Roll 710, Page 175; GSU microfilm available

1860 Ohio Census — Meigs County, Salisbury Township (as young woman)

Head: Mary AULT, female, age 55, born New Jersey
Eliza AULT (daughter), female, age 18, born Ohio
Household also includes:

Alex GILMORE, male, age 26, born Ohio
Hettie GILMORE, female, age 22, born Ohio
Charles GILMORE, male, age 4, born Ohio


Source: Series M653, Roll 1008, Page 34; GSU microfilm available

1880 Iowa Census — Cherokee County, Afton Township (as wife)

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, married, age 39, occupation farmer, born Ohio
Elizabeth TALLMAN (wife), female, married, age 30, born Ohio, occupation keeping house
Children:

Willis G. TALLMAN, male, single, age 6, born Iowa
Lena TALLMAN, female, single, age 1, born Iowa


Household also includes:

Elbert WINN, male, single, age 17, born Iowa
Mary AULT (mother), female, widow, age 73, born Maine, occupation aged
Andrew RICHEY, male, single, age 24, born Illinois, occupation farm laborer


Source: Fam Hist Lib Film 1254332, Page 52D; GSU microfilm available

1900 Iowa Census — Woodbury County, Sioux City, p. 231R, Filmore Avenue

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, birthdate Apr 1841, age 58, occupation teamster
Elizabeth P. TALLMAN (wife), female, race White, birthdate Oct 1841, age 58
Child:

Lena P. TALLMAN, female, race White, birthdate Feb 1879, age 21


Source: Series T623, Roll 467, Page 231B; GSU microfilm available

1910 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Linn Township, Iowa Soldiers’ Home

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, age 69, occupation none
Elizabeth P. TALLMAN (wife), female, race White, age 68
Note: Residing at Iowa Soldiers’ Home; no children listed
Source: Series T624, Roll 441, Page 72; GSU microfilm available

1920 Iowa Census — Marshall County, Iowa Soldiers’ Home

Head: Miller M. TALLMAN, male, race White, age 78, occupation none, member of Soldiers’ Home
Elizabeth TALLMAN (wife), female, race White, age 78, member of Soldiers’ Home
Note: Continued residence at Iowa Soldiers’ Home; household status: married couple
Source: Series T625, Roll 502, Pages 5B, ED 155; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Father: Frederick Ault (1795-c.1857), carpenter in Ohio
Mother: Mary Ault (b. ~1802 Massachusetts)
Siblings: Charles B. Ault, Mehitable Ault
Husband: Miller Mathias Tallman (1841-1921), farmer and military veteran
Children identified: Willis G. Tallman (b. ~1874), Lena/Lena P. Tallman (b. 1879)
Pedigree significance: Bridges Ault family of Ohio with Tallman family expansion into Iowa; extended family included Mary Ault (mother) in 1880 household

Family Diagram

graph TD
  FA["Frederick Ault<br/>1795-c.1857<br/>Carpenter, Ohio"]
  MA["Mary Ault<br/>b. ~1802<br/>Massachusetts"]
  EPA["Elizabeth Plomey Ault<br/>1841-1926"]

  LT["Lena P. Tallman<br/>1879-?"]
  
  FA --> EPA
  MA --> EPA
  EPA --> MMT
  EPA --> WG
  EPA --> LT

Elizabeth Plomey bridges three states and five decades: Ohio childhood (1850-1860), Iowa farming marriage (1880-1900), Sioux City teamster’s household (1900), and shared Iowa Soldiers’ Home residence with husband (1910-1920).

Locate Willis G. Tallman in later census records to trace his life.
Trace Lena P. Tallman’s life after 1900 and identify her spouse/descendants.
Validate exact marriage date (c. 1880) from marriage records.
Clarify relationship of Andrew Richey and Elbert Winn in the 1880 household.
Confirm Iowa Soldiers’ Home admission reason and military service timeline for Miller M. Tallman.

People/Lester-Harold-Spicer.title: Lester Harold Spicer
People/Lester-Harold-Spicer.content:
Lester Harold Spicer
Biographical Profile

Name: Lester Harold Spicer
Birth/Death: Born 14 Jul 1906; died 28 Jun 1974 (age 67 years, 11 months, 14 days)
Role in this project: Third-generation Spicer; son of George B. Spicer (farmer); transition from agricultural to industrial occupation (car repairer); married Ruby Bernice Prior.
Burial: Cedar Memorial Cemetery, Cedar Rapids, Iowa; 64K Last Supper, Space 4; GPS 42°1’24.2”N 91°38’17.4”W; inscription LESTER H. / 1906 † 1974; Burial Sites book, page 33.

Census Records and Life Progression
1910 Iowa Census — Benton County, Canton Township (as young child)

Head: George Spicer, male, age 45, farmer
Mother: Hattie Spicer, female, age 32
Lester H. Spicer appears as son, male, age 3
Siblings: Edith (9), Charles R. (8), George G. (6), Chester J. (2.3)
Uncle: William Spicer, male, age 49 (farmer laborer)
Source: Series T624, Roll 391, Pages 56-57

1920 Iowa Census — Benton County, Benton Township (as adolescent)

Head: George Spicer, male, age 55, farmer
Mother: Hattie Spicer, female, age 42
Lester H. Spicer appears as son, male, age 13
Siblings: Charles R. (17, farm laborer), George J. (15, farm laborer), Chester J. (11), Edna (7), Myron (4+)
Note: Older brothers already working as farm laborers; Lester still in school/home
Source: Series T625, Roll 477, Pages 4B & 5A, ED 4

1930 Iowa Census — Linn County, Clinton Township (as young adult)

Head: George Spicer, male, age 65, farmer
Mother: Hattie Spicer, female, age 53
Lester H. Spicer, male, age 23, car repairer (railroad shop) — occupational shift away from agriculture
Siblings: Chester J. (21, farmer), Edna (17), Myron L. (14)
Source: Series T626, Roll 664, Page 11A, ED 8

1940 Iowa Census — Linn County, Cedar Rapids, 17th Ward (as adult)

Head: Lester H. Spicer, male, age 32, torch operator, rock crushing manufacturer
Wife: Ruby B. Spicer, female, age 26
Son: Richard Spicer, male, age 4
Address: 724 Seventh Street SW, Cedar Rapids
Note: Established in town with manufacturing job; one child; urban rather than rural residence
Source: Series T9, Roll [unlisted], Page [unlisted], ED [unlisted]

Family Connections and Life Trajectory


Father: George B. Spicer (1864-1938) — patriarch farmer


Mother: Hattie May Risden (1877-1967) — daughter of Sarah Annett Lemmon/Monroe Thorpe


Wife: Ruby Bernice Prior — married by 1940


Son: Richard Spicer (age 4 in 1940)


Siblings: Edith, Charles Russell, George G., Chester J., Edna, Myron L.


Occupational Transition
Lester Harold Spicer represents the agricultural-to-industrial transition of the 1920s-1940s:

1910-1920: Child of farmer, in agricultural household
1920: Age 13, farming household (brothers working as farm laborers)
1930: Age 23, occupational pivot — car repairer for railroad (skilled industrial trade, not farm labor)
1940: Age 32, torch operator in rock crushing factory; established in Cedar Rapids (urban center)

Broader Spicer Line Context
Four-generation progression:

Nathan Spicer (1796-1873): Patriarch, blacksmith/farm laborer origin
Charles Russell Spicer (1822-1887): Son, farmer/chair maker transition
George B. Spicer (1864-1938): Grandson, prosperous farmer with 7+ children
Lester Harold Spicer (1906-1974): Great-grandson, industrial worker; leaves agriculture

Research Gaps

Confirm marriage date to Ruby Bernice Prior
Clarify Richard Spicer’s full parentage (1940 census shows him at age 4)
Verify torch operator/rock crushing manufacturer position (likely Central Iowa cement/gravel operations)
Confirm death date and location from vital records

Biographical Profile

Family Connections


Children identified:

Henry Spicer (son, age 20 in 1850, Ohio-born)
Zura Spicer (daughter, age 18 in 1850, Ohio-born)
William Spicer (son, major farmer with 1500 acres in 1860-1870)
Charles Russell Spicer (son? — lineage suggests Nathan → Charles Russell → George B.)



Son William Spicer: Farmer, estate valued $1500, supports aging father in 1860s


Grandchildren: Through William and Delilah: Mary C. Spicer visible in 1860


Household trajectory:

1850: Aging patriarch (54) visiting daughter Maria’s blacksmith household
1860: Dependent in son William’s farming household (age 64, farm laborer)
1870: Fully dependent elderly (age 74, “without prospect”) in son’s household



Burial and Legacy

Burial: Spicer/Spooner Cemetery, Homestead, Iowa (same cemetery where George B. Spicer’s wife Hattie May Risden was eventually buried)
Legacy: Patriarch of Spicer line spanning 77 years (1796-1873); established in Iowa by 1850; sons continued agricultural prosperity (William with 1500-acre farm by 1860)

Three-Generation Spicer Progression

Nathan Spicer (1796-1873): Patriarch, emigrated from New York/Ohio; established in Iowa by 1850
Charles Russell Spicer (1822-1887): Son; farmer, chair maker, bridge generation
George B. Spicer (1864-1938): Grandson; major patriarch with large household, 7+ children; prosperous farmer

Source: Series M432, Roll 1004, Page 150; GSU microfilm available

Not found in 1880 census; presumed deceased between 1860-1880 (Oliver Lewis’s 1880 household does not include Susan).

Status: CONFIRMED — Susan Lewis’s identity across 1850-1860 verified as single individual based on:

Consecutive census entries (1850, 1860) with consistent family connections
Same household network (Wynat Lewis, Oliver Lewis) across both censuses

Death inference: Not listed in 1880 household; presumed deceased between 1860-1880

Note: Birthplace varies (Vermont 1850, NY 1860) — likely census data quality variation, not identity issue.

William Henry Palmer
Biographical Profile

Name: William Henry Palmer
Role in this project: Palmer-line patriarch bridging Wisconsin (childhood), Minnesota (farming), and Iowa (final decades); son of John K Palmer; father of May Aleen Palmer.

Source-Cited Facts

Birth/Death: Born 12 Jun 1851 (inscribed on headstone); died 8 May 1927, age 75, in Waterloo, Iowa.
Cause of Death: Acute bronchial pneumonia (death-index entry).
Burial: Fairview Cemetery, Waterloo, Iowa; section M, Row 3, Grave 67; inscription WILLIAM H. / JUNE 12, 1851 / MAY 8, 1927 / FATHER.

Census Records and Life Progression
1860 Wisconsin Census — Sauk County, Baraboo (as child)

Head: J PALMER (John K Palmer), male, age 38, farmer
William H. Palmer (son), male, age 9, born Pennsylvania
Source: Series M653, Roll 1429, Page 450; GSU microfilm available

1900 Minnesota Census — Mower County, Frankford Township

Head: William PALMER, male, race White, birthdate Jan 1851, age 49, born Pennsylvania, occupation farmer
Wife: Elizabeth PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Sep 1857, age 42, born Wisconsin, occupation none
Children:

Louis PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Oct 1884, age 16, born Wisconsin
May PALMER, female, race White, birthdate May 1887, age 13, born Wisconsin (later May Aleen Palmer)
Dott PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Aug 1889, age 11, born Wisconsin
Ivas PALMER, female, race White, birthdate Sep 1891, age 8, born Wisconsin
Voila PALMER, female, race White, birthdate May 1896, age 4, born Wisconsin
Vivian PALMER, female, race White, birthdate MAY 1898, age 2, born Minnesota


Source: Series T623, Roll 777, Page 3A; GSU microfilm available

1920 Iowa Census — Blackhawk County, Waterloo, Summer Street

Head: Viola McMillLEN, female, age 28, widow, seamstress/garment factory worker
Household members:

Bernice McMillLEN, female, age 25 (stepdaughter)
William PALMER, male, age 68, father, no occupation
Elizabeth PALMER, female, age 65, mother, no occupation
Maxine WALTON, female, age 6 (stepdaughter)


Note: William and Elizabeth Palmer living with daughter Viola’s household in Iowa by 1920
Source: Series T625, Roll 478, Pages 24B, ED 33; GSU microfilm available

Family Connections

Father: John K Palmer (1821-1906), Wisconsin farmer
Wife: Elizabeth Palmer (b. Sep 1857 Wisconsin)
Children identified: Vern (b. ~1882), Louis (b. ~1884), May/May Aleen (b. 1887, married Oliver Prior), Dott (b. ~1889), Ivas (b. ~1891), Voila (b. ~1896), Vivian (b. ~1898)
Daughter Viola married, and William and Elizabeth moved to Waterloo to be near family in 1910s-1920s
Pedigree significance: Bridges Wisconsin Palmer (father’s line) to Minnesota farming expansion and Iowa family consolidation

Source file: References/raw/butch-thorpe-email.txt
Sender and Context

Sender signs as Butch (nickname for Robert “Butch” Thorpe).
Message sent to Zach with interpretation guidance for attached pedigree timeline files.

Extracted Statements

Attachments are intended as a resource-finding aid and include pedigrees.
Horizontal bars represent ancestor lifespans.
Not all ancestors with records are shown; only those from earliest generations that fit on the page.
Red dots denote official record types, with nearby numbers referencing Butch’s personal copies.
Blue dots denote census records at timeline dates.
Butch can provide a PDF describing the record types he has collected.

Notes
This email provides interpretation metadata for the .cdr pedigree files and should be cited when describing how timeline markers are encoded.

Coverage: Primarily UK censuses (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13 series)

Multiple census year entries with locations

Context

These notes compile citation references for UK and selected US census appearances tied to Thorpe-line ancestors.

Extracted Statements

The notes include UK citation references for 1841, 1861, 1871, and 1901 census years using Class/Piece/Folio/Page formats (for example HO107, RG9, RG10, RG13).
Individuals listed include Bellamy, Sorrell, Thorogood, Emblow, Kelly, Whitfield, and Munson/Monson variants.
The notes include US citation entries for 1900 with roll/page/enumeration district formats and names including Clyde Thorpe and Joseph Prior.
English Census Notes.txt links individual image filenames to citation metadata, indicating potential image-level validation paths.

Person-name spelling varies across files, including Monson/Munson and Witfield/Whitfield.
Notes include possible or inferred matches in some filename annotations and should be treated as provisional until image checks are complete.

References/Shared-Intake-2026-04-22-Cens.content:

Source files:

References/raw/inbox/2026-04-22-intake/Census/EnglishCensusCitations.txt

Processing scope

Parsed and reviewed pages 1-10 of CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf.
This is a partial extraction pass focused on high-confidence entries in early pages.

Extracted statements

The document title page identifies the work as a census summary for individual ancestors of Lancelot and Wendolyn Thorpe, compiled by Robert Thorpe in October 2009.
The introduction states the intended census coverage range is 1841 through 1930 and that the index lists direct ancestors.
The contents pages include direct-ancestor entries for AULT, Andrew, AULT, Elizabeth Plomey, and AULT, Frederick.
Page 9 includes an 1850 Island Creek Township, Jefferson County, Ohio household for Andrew Ault (age 82, farmer, born New York) with Elizabeth Ault (age 83, born New York) and Amy Palmer (age 17, born Ohio), cited to Series M432, Roll 699, Page 523.
Page 10 includes an Elizabeth Plomey Ault section with census entries for 1850, 1860, 1880, 1900, 1910, and 1920, including repeated Tallman-household entries for Miller M. Tallman and Elizabeth P. Tallman.
Page 10 entries identify Lena/Lena H. Tallman as daughter in 1880 and 1900 records and include birthplace fields and household relationships.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing may contain minor transcription artifacts in abbreviations and column headers.
Extracted statements are limited to pages 1-10 and do not yet include deeper person-specific pages after page 10.
Compiler contact details visible in the introduction were not propagated to person pages.

Processing scope

Parsed and reviewed pages 11-20 of CensusSummaryIndividual.pdf.
This extraction pass covers entries from Ault, Bangle, Baxter/Rowland, Bellamy, Beneworth/Thorogood, and Blake/Lemmon sections.

Extracted statements

Page 11 includes AULT, Frederick with the 1850 Salisbury Township, Meigs County, Ohio household and citation Series M432, Roll 710, Page 175.
Pages 12-13 include BANGLE, Elizabeth Maria and BANGLE, Louis S Jr with recurring 1860 Michigan and 1870/1880 Wisconsin household entries and citations including M653 Roll 1407 and M593 Roll 1712.
Page 14 includes BAXTER, Matilda with 1850 Indiana and 1860/1870 Iowa Rowland household entries and citation Series M432, Roll 154, Page 241.
Pages 15-18 include Bellamy branch entries (Henry James, James, James Archibald, Richard) with repeated 19th/early 20th century Lincolnshire/Peterborough census extracts and multiple UK source references (HO107, RG9, RG10, RG11, RG12, RG13, RG14 tokens as listed).
Page 19 includes BENEWORTH, Hannah with 1841/1861 Thorogood-linked entries in Felsted, Essex.
Page 20 includes BLAKE, Rebecca with an 1850 Townsend Township, Sandusky County, Ohio Lemmon household entry and citation Series M432, Roll 726, Page 476.

Uncertainty notes

OCR/parsing introduces visible spelling and formatting inconsistencies in some names and place strings.
Several UK entries on these pages contain incomplete piece/folio/page fields in the compiled table text and require image-level verification.
Some household role interpretations are compiler-normalized and should be treated as provisional until direct page-image checks are complete.

Topics/Ault-Tallman-Branch-Summary.title: Ault Tallman Branch Summary
Topics/Ault-Tallman-Branch-Summary.content:
Ault and Tallman Branch Summary
This branch is a strong visitor path because it has a clear household story: Ohio Ault records, Iowa Tallman farming households, and later Soldiers’ Home and cemetery evidence. It also connects to the landing-page Tallman monument image.
Branch Diagram

graph TD
  FA["Frederick Ault<br/>1795-c.1857"]
  EPA["Elizabeth Plomey Ault<br/>1841-1926"]
  MMT["Miller Mathias Tallman<br/>1841-1921"]
  BT["Benjamin B Tallman<br/>1811-1881"]
  RM["Romancy Miller<br/>1819-1911"]
  WG["Willis G. Tallman<br/>b. ~1874"]
  LT["Lena P. Tallman<br/>1879-?"]

  FA --> EPA
  EPA --- MMT
  EPA --> WG
  EPA --> LT
  BT --- RM
  BT -. "possible related Tallman cluster" .-> MMT

The solid family group around Elizabeth Plomey Ault and Miller Mathias Tallman is documented in census summaries. The Benjamin/Romancy connection is shown as a nearby Tallman cluster, not as a proved direct relationship to Miller Mathias Tallman.
Start With These People

Elizabeth Plomey Ault - Ohio-to-Iowa life progression from 1850 through 1920.
Miller Mathias Tallman - Elizabeth’s husband, Iowa farmer/teamster and later Soldiers’ Home resident.
Frederick Ault - Ohio Ault household head and father of Elizabeth Plomey Ault.
Benjamin B Tallman - Iowa Tallman farming patriarch with 1850-1880 household coverage.
Romancy Miller - Tallman matriarch tracked across six decades of household records.

What We Know

Elizabeth Plomey Ault appears as a child and young woman in Ohio Ault households, then as Elizabeth Tallman in Iowa census records.
Miller Mathias Tallman and Elizabeth are documented in 1880, 1900, 1910, and 1920 census summaries, including Iowa Soldiers’ Home records.
Benjamin B Tallman and Romancy Miller are documented across Iowa household records from 1850 through 1880, with Romancy continuing into later widowhood records.
Burial-site evidence supports several dates and cemetery placements in this family cluster.

What Remains Uncertain

The relationship between the Benjamin/Romancy Tallman household and Miller Mathias Tallman remains a research gap.
Some children in the Tallman households need later-life tracing, especially Willis G. Tallman and Lena P. Tallman.
Original census images should be reviewed to validate OCR-sensitive spellings such as Tallman, Talmon, and Tollman.

This page ranks the unresolved identity questions that can still be improved with the source material already present in the vault.

The diagram is a visual index of the current queue. It does not imply any merge beyond what is written in the table below.
Ranking Key

High: Strong source-text support already exists; image-level confirmation is the main missing step.
Medium: The source text is useful but still leaves a meaningful ambiguity or competing interpretation.
Low: The current source set supports only a tentative note, not even a cautious merge candidate.

Matrix

Image verification pending (GSU rolls available).Eleanor EmblowRESOLVED30-year census sequence (1841-1871) as wife of Joseph Emblow; name variants (Eleanor/Elenor/Ellen) consolidated; duplicate “Eleanor Ireland” page deleted.✓ Identity confirmed and consolidated.Sarah KellyRESOLVED20-year census sequence (1841-1861) as household member, widow, and almshouse resident; consistent birthplace (Cambridgeshire, Whittlesey); clear family relationship to son James Kelly.✓ Identity confirmed via source text. Death date 1860-1880 requires Wisconsin vital records.Mary GreenwoodVERIFIED SEPARATE1850 Iowa mortality schedule (age 38, died March) vs Mary Burgett (born 1835, died 1918). Age discrepancy (38 vs 15 in 1850) and different death dates confirm separate identities.✓ Correctly kept as separate pages. Both pages cross-reference and explain distinction.Wynat LewisRESOLVEDTwo-census sequence (1850-1860) with consistent household (wife Susan, children Oliver, Martha); age progression 68→79 matches 10-year span; pedigree timeline provides canonical form (Wynant Williamson Lewis).✓ Identity confirmed and canonical form established as Wynant Williamson Lewis. Topics/Lemmon-Blake-Thorpe-Branch-Summar.content:
Lemmon, Blake, and Thorpe Branch Summary
This is the core Thorpe-connected branch hub. It explains why the Lemmon, Blake, and Thorpe pages are linked in the vault while keeping the evidence boundaries clear: the compiled pedigree timeline places these families in the same branch context, but individual relationships still need record-level confirmation in places.
Branch Diagram

graph TD
  HM["Hugh Lemmon<br/>1754-1824"]
  JL["James Lemmon<br/>1779-1854"]
  RB["Rebecca Blake<br/>1779-1855"]
  UBL["Uriah Blake Lemmon<br/>1808-1887"]
  SAL["Sarah Annett Lemmon<br/>1841-1886"]
  UBT["Uriah Blake Thorpe<br/>1878-1959"]
  HMR["Hattie May Risden<br/>1877-1967"]

  HM --> JL
  JL --- RB
  JL --> UBL
  RB --> UBL
  UBL --> SAL
  SAL --> UBT
  SAL --> HMR

This diagram is a visitor guide, not a standalone proof. It summarizes relationships and branch placement already discussed in the linked pages and in the extracted Thorpe pedigree timeline.
Start With These People

Uriah Blake Thorpe - later Iowa Thorpe identity, verified as separate from Uriah Blake Lemmon.
Uriah Blake Lemmon - earlier Ohio Lemmon identity in the same compiled branch context.
James Lemmon and Rebecca Blake - the Lemmon/Blake couple anchoring the older branch layer.
Sarah Annett Lemmon - visible bridge from Lemmon naming into later Thorpe household naming.
Hattie May Risden - descendant-line profile that connects the Lemmon/Thorpe context into the Spicer-Risden family story.

What We Know

The extracted Thorpe pedigree timeline places Uriah Blake Thorpe, Uriah Blake Lemmon, James Lemmon, Rebecca Blake, and Sarah Annett Lemmon in a shared branch context.
Uriah Blake Thorpe and Uriah Blake Lemmon are verified as separate people: they differ by about 70 years in birth date and have different death and burial contexts.
The Blake surname is part of the Lemmon ancestral context through Rebecca Blake, not a reason to merge the two Uriah Blake identities.
Sarah Annett Lemmon is the most useful bridge page for understanding how this branch connects forward into the later Thorpe/Risden/Spicer cluster.

What Remains Uncertain

Some early-generation placements still come from the compiled pedigree timeline and need image-level or record-level verification.
The exact relationship path between all Lemmon, Blake, Thorpe, and Risden pages should remain source-limited until additional vital records or image-verified census records are reviewed.
Several older names visible in the timeline, including Hugh Lemmon and related allied-family names, do not yet have full person pages in the vault.

Start With These People

Wynant Williamson Lewis - early Lewis household head whose name variants have been reconciled.
Oliver Warren Lewis - Lewis farmer tracked from Wisconsin to Minnesota.
Martha Eliza Lewis - Lewis-to-Prior bridge profile with census records from 1860 through 1920.

What We Know

Wynat Lewis has been reconciled as Wynant Williamson Lewis, with 1850-1860 Wisconsin household continuity and pedigree-timeline support.
Oliver Warren Lewis and Martha Eliza Lewis show a Lewis branch moving from Vermont/Wisconsin context into Minnesota.
Martha Eliza Lewis and Arthur Edwin Prior anchor a Prior household that appears in Minnesota, South Dakota, and Minnesota again across 1900-1920.
May Aleen Palmer documents the Palmer-to-Prior marriage path and a large Cedar Rapids household in 1920-1930.

What Remains Uncertain

Arthur Edwin Prior still has a birth-year conflict between census-summary evidence and burial/timeline evidence.
Several Prior and Palmer household extracts contain OCR ambiguity in child names, occupations, or repeated rows.

Resolved Identity Merges

Pending Reconciliations


UNKNOWN placeholder identities mapped by census cross references

UNKNOWN, Ann is cross-referenced from SORREL, Ann and appears in extraction household entries as Ann SORREL/SORRELL.
UNKNOWN, Eleanor is cross-referenced from EMBLOW, Eleanor/Elenor/Ellen and appears in extraction household entries as spouse of Joseph Emblow.
UNKNOWN, Sarah (Barton?) is cross-referenced from KELLEY, Sarah and KELLY, Sarah and appears in extraction household entries as Sarah KELLY in Peterborough almshouse records.

Canonical profiles have now been created for tracking and future merge finalization:

Ann Sorrell
Eleanor Emblow
Sarah Kelly
Susan Lewis


The canonical pages now include page-level anchors from References/raw/extracted/CensusSummaryIndividual.txt, but image-level verification is still pending for the entries with missing piece/folio/page fields.
Transitional alias pages retained for traceability:

Ann Unknown
Eleanor Unknown
Sarah Unknown
Susan Unknown





Mary Greenwood vs Mary Bergett

The 1850 mortality schedule entry for Mary Bergett (died March 1850, age 38) is tracked on Mary Greenwood using the compiler’s GREENWOOD, Mary (c. 1812 - Mar 1850) heading.
This is a separate identity from Mary Burgett (1835-1918); do not merge them.
Preserve GREENWOOD as compiler metadata if a cleaner canonical title is needed later.



Uriah Blake Thorpe vs Uriah Blake Lemmon

The Thorpe pedigree timeline places Uriah Blake Thorpe (1878-1959), Uriah Blake Lemmon (1808-1887), James Lemmon (1779-1854), Rebecca Blake (1779-1855), and Sarah Annett Lemmon (1841-1886) on the same compiled branch chart. That supports a branch-level connection, but not a direct identity merge between the two Uriahs.



Wynat vs William Lewis

The census-summary index lists LEWIS, Wynat (William?), but the pedigree timeline names the person Wynant Williamson Lewis c1781-after 1860. The best-supported in-repo full form is therefore Wynant Williamson Lewis, while William remains an unconfirmed expansion of the OCR/compiler shorthand. Reconcile Wynat Lewis once primary image records are reviewed.
A Welsh etymology is not supported by the current source set. Treat Wynat as an uncertain spelling form until a primary record or family paper supports a different reading.



Action Plan for Batch Ingestion

Use canonical forms for page titles but preserve exact spelling from source in the profile body.
Cross-link all variant-spelled sources to the canonical person page.
Update People Directory and Search Index to point to canonical pages with “See also” or alias notes if necessary.

Topics/Spicer-Risden-Branch-Summary.content:
Spicer and Risden Branch Summary
This branch follows an Iowa-centered Spicer family story from nineteenth-century farming households into twentieth-century industrial work. It is one of the most visitor-ready parts of the vault because several profiles already include repeated census households, burial-site notes, and compact family diagrams.
Branch Diagram

graph TD
  NS["Nathan Spicer<br/>1796-1873<br/>Iowa patriarch"]
  CRS["Charles Russell Spicer<br/>1822-1887<br/>farmer / chair maker"]
  GBS["George B. Spicer<br/>1864-1938<br/>Iowa farmer"]
  HMR["Hattie May Risden<br/>1877-1967<br/>Lemmon/Thorpe connection"]
  LHS["Lester Harold Spicer<br/>1906-1974<br/>industrial worker"]
  RBP["Ruby Bernice Prior<br/>1913-2006"]

  NS -. "lineage lead" .-> CRS
  CRS -. "relationship needs confirmation" .-> GBS
  GBS --- HMR
  GBS --> LHS
  HMR --> LHS
  LHS --- RBP

Dashed arrows mark lineage links that are useful for navigation but still need direct record confirmation.
Start With These People

Nathan Spicer - early Spicer patriarch documented in 1850-1870 Iowa census households and burial-site notes.
Charles Russell Spicer - bridge-generation Spicer profile with census households from 1850-1880.
George B. Spicer - Iowa farmer and household head whose page anchors the later Spicer family.
Hattie May Risden - connects the Spicer story back to the Lemmon/Thorpe branch.
Lester Harold Spicer and Ruby Bernice Prior - later-generation couple linking the Spicer and Prior lines.

What We Know

Nathan Spicer appears across 1850, 1860, and 1870 Iowa census summaries, moving from a blacksmith-related household to dependent residence with family.
George B. Spicer is documented as a child or young adult in 1870 and 1880, then as a household head with Hattie May Risden in 1900-1920.
Lester Harold Spicer is tracked from child in George and Hattie’s farming household to railroad and manufacturing work in Cedar Rapids.
The Spicer/Spooner and Spring Grove cemetery entries provide burial context for several members of this branch.

What Remains Uncertain

The exact Nathan-to-Charles-to-George relationship chain remains a research gap on the person pages and should not be treated as fully proved.
Some census extracts contain OCR or compiler ambiguity, especially around household relationships and name forms.
The Ruby Bernice Prior connection is source-backed by staged lineage, census summaries, the Prior timeline, and burial notes, but her page still flags parentage correlation as an open task.

index.title: Thorpe Family History
index.content:
Thorpe Family History

This site gathers the Thorpe family history shared by Robert “Butch” Thorpe and turns it into a source-grounded family wiki. It follows the Thorpe line through connected Lemmon, Blake, Spicer, Risden, Palmer, Prior, Lewis, Ault, and Tallman families.
The best way to begin is with one of the branch stories below. Each branch page gives a plain-language overview, a compact family diagram, links to the strongest person profiles, and a short list of what still needs verification.
Start With a Family Branch

























BranchWhat to expectLemmon, Blake, and ThorpeThe core Thorpe-connected branch context from the compiled pedigree timeline, including the two separate Uriah Blake identities and the Sarah Annett Lemmon bridge into the Thorpe line.Spicer and RisdenAn Iowa farming line from Nathan Spicer through George B. Spicer, Hattie May Risden, and Lester Harold Spicer.Palmer, Prior, and LewisA migration story from Wisconsin and Minnesota into Iowa, connecting Wynant Williamson Lewis, Martha Eliza Lewis, May Aleen Palmer, and the Prior family.Ault and TallmanOhio-to-Iowa household records for the Ault and Tallman families, including Elizabeth Plomey Ault, Miller Mathias Tallman, Benjamin B Tallman, and Romancy Miller.
Explore by Need

Find a person: Use the People Directory for the full alphabetical list.
Search by topic or source: Use the Search Index.
See the source trail: Start with Butch Thorpe Email, Thorpe Pedigree Timelines, and Census InDesign Summaries.

What This Site Is
This is a research wiki, not a finished family book. Some pages are well supported by census summaries, burial records, and pedigree timeline exports; other pages are still placeholders for future verification. When the evidence is uncertain, the page should say so.
The current vault documents 111 people and includes census extracts, burial-site notes, compiled pedigree timelines, and references to 31 genealogical or historical book extracts. The strongest visitor-ready pages are linked from the branch summaries above.
